This closes the round-0 HIGH finding: the fixed entry 14 discriminates correctly in both the single-violation and multi-violation case, unlike the original ls-based command which could never fail short of all 8 forbidden files existing simultaneously.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. The sole round-0 HIGH finding (verification entry 14's ls-based multi-argument false-pass) is independently re-verified as closed, not merely trusted from the generator's design_notes.rev1_summary claim: the revised per-file `for`/`[ -e ]` loop correctly fails and names the offending path when exactly one forbidden ecosystem/*.md document is created (the evaluator's own round-0 probe, re-run here), correctly fails and names ALL offending paths when three forbidden documents are created simultaneously (a stronger multi-document probe added in this round, beyond what the generator's own revision claims to have tested), and correctly returns to PASS after each probe is fully reverted with no residue. Structural, position-by-position diff against a reconstruction of the reviewed round-0 contract confirms `scope`, `non_goals`, `estimated_lines`, `evidence_convention`, `evidence_dir`, `name`, and `spec_ref` are all byte-identical, and 13 of the 14 verification entries are byte-identical verbatim -- only entry 14 was changed, exactly as claimed and nothing more. The full 14-entry suite was re-extracted from the live contract and re-run from the repository root against the pre-implementation tree: entries 1/3/4/5/6/7/8 correctly fail (target file absent), entries 2/9/10/11/12/13/14 all pass, matching the round-0 pass/fail pattern with entry 14 now passing on sound rather than vacuous logic. The .agent/ immutability guard (entry 11) was additionally confirmed tolerant of a REAL, externally-produced concurrent feature-032 qa artifact that appeared in the shared tree since round 0 (not just a synthetic same-session probe), closing any residual doubt about the concurrency-fence's practical correctness. The contract is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json. One minor, non-blocking, cosmetic issue was noted: the 'entries 3-9'->'entries 3-8' prose correction was appended to the wrong design_notes field (verification entry 2's own acceptance_test text, the actual location of the imprecise range, remains unedited) -- this does not affect any verification command's logic and was already treated as non-blocking in round 0. The repository was left byte-for-byte unmodified by this round's review activity (git status and git diff HEAD identical before/after; all probes reverted; no 032 surface touched; .agent/run_state.json untouched). Cleared to advance to Loop-2 implementation.
